differentials.name: homogeneous sclerotic mass typically without hemorrhage / necrosis
differentials.raw: homogeneous sclerotic mass typically without hemorrhage / necrosis

differentials.name: central portion with variable degree of fibrosis + coagulative necrosis with scanty tumor cells (b)   periductal infiltrating / diffuse type:
differentials.raw: central portion with variable degree of fibrosis + coagulative necrosis with scanty tumor cells (b)   periductal infiltrating / diffuse type:

differentials.name: elongated spiculated / branching growth along dilated / narrowed bile duct (c)  intraductal tubular polypoid / papillary type:
differentials.raw: elongated spiculated / branching growth along dilated / narrowed bile duct (c)  intraductal tubular polypoid / papillary type:

differentials.name: infrequent slow growing tumor with relatively favorable prognosis
differentials.raw: infrequent slow growing tumor with relatively favorable prognosis

differentials.name: frequently associated with marked mucin production (d)  mixed = combination of mass-forming + periductal Histo:   well / moderately / poorly differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma (most common) / papillary / mucinous / signet-ring cell / mucoepidermoid / adenosquamous adenocarcinoma with abundant fibrous stroma
differentials.raw: frequently associated with marked mucin production (d)  mixed = combination of mass-forming + periductal Histo:   well / moderately / poorly differentiated ductal adenocarcinoma (most common) / papillary / mucinous / signet-ring cell / mucoepidermoid / adenosquamous adenocarcinoma with abundant fibrous stroma

differentials.name: Squamous cell carcinoma =  metaplastic transformation of adenocarcinoma
differentials.raw: Squamous cell carcinoma =  metaplastic transformation of adenocarcinoma

differentials.name: Inflammatory bowel disease
differentials.raw: Inflammatory bowel disease (10 x increased risk); incidence of 0.4-1.4% in ulcerative colitis; latent period of 15 years; tumors usually multicentric + predominantly in extrahepatic sites; GB involved in 15% (simultaneous presence of gallstones is rare)

differentials.name: Infection with liver flukes: Opisthorchis viverrini and Clonorchis sinensis infestation (Far East) ◊  Most common cause worldwide!
differentials.raw: Infection with liver flukes: Opisthorchis viverrini and Clonorchis sinensis infestation (Far East) ◊  Most common cause worldwide!

differentials.name: Viral infection: HIV, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, Epstein-Barr virus
differentials.raw: Viral infection: HIV, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, Epstein-Barr virus

differentials.name: Congenital biliary cystic disease: choledochal cyst / congenital hepatic cyst / congenital biliary atresia
differentials.raw: Congenital biliary cystic disease: choledochal cyst / congenital hepatic cyst / congenital biliary atresia

differentials.name: gradual onset of fluctuating painless jaundice
differentials.raw: gradual onset of fluctuating painless jaundice

differentials.name: intermittent epigastric pain, enlarged tender liver
differentials.raw: intermittent epigastric pain, enlarged tender liver

differentials.name: Metastatic adenocarcinoma
differentials.raw: Metastatic adenocarcinoma (central necrosis = strongly T2-hyperintense + T1- hypointense)

differentials.name: Hemangioma
differentials.raw: Hemangioma (strong globular enhancement, NO ragged rim)

differentials.name: Organizing abscess
differentials.raw: Organizing abscess (thick enhancing wall with central cystic change)

context: Path (Japanese Liver Cancer Study Group): (a)  mass-forming (= nodular / exophytic) type: commonly in peripheral cholangiocarcinoma commonly in hilar + extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma Unusual manifestation: √  severe diffuse dilatation of intra- and extrahepatic bile ducts proximal + distal to tumor Genetics:   mutation in p53 tumor suppressor gene + k-ras gene Peak prevalence:   7th decade; M > F Predisposed:   conditions causing chronic biliary inflammation ›  Biliary hamartoma ›  Autosomal dominant polycystic disease ›  Congenital hepatic fibrosis ›  Caroli disease ← chronic biliary stasis
